seoTitle: AMEC Semiconductor Manufacturing Solutions | Integrated Process Solutions
seoDescription: Complete semiconductor manufacturing solutions from AMEC including advanced logic, 3D NAND, and LED manufacturing. Integrated equipment and process support from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What types of manufacturing solutions does AMEC provide?
faqs.answer: AMEC provides comprehensive manufacturing solutions including: 1) Advanced Logic Manufacturing Solution - complete etching and deposition solution for FinFET and GAA transistor fabrication down to 3nm nodes. 2) 3D NAND Manufacturing Solution - high aspect ratio etching solution for memory device manufacturing with >100:1 aspect ratio capability. 3) LED Manufacturing Solution - MOCVD-based solution for high-volume LED production. 4) Custom Process Solutions - tailored solutions for specific applications and requirements. All solutions include equipment, process development, integration support, and ongoing technical assistance from AMEC and authorized distributor LiTong Electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select solution based on your device type and manufacturing requirements. Contact FAE for custom solution consultation.

faqs.question: What is included in AMEC's Integrated Fab Solution?
faqs.answer: The Integrated Fab Solution is a turnkey offering that includes: 1) Equipment supply - all AMEC equipment required for the application including etching, deposition, and MOCVD systems. 2) Process development - development of optimized recipes for all process steps. 3) Equipment integration - design and implementation of integrated equipment configuration. 4) Installation and commissioning - complete installation and startup at customer site. 5) Training - comprehensive training for operators and maintenance personnel. 6) Documentation - complete process documentation and procedures. 7) Ongoing support - technical support throughout production lifecycle. The solution is customized for each customer's specific requirements and includes performance guarantees.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated Fab Solution provides comprehensive support for new fab setup or major expansion. Ideal for risk mitigation.

faqs.question: How does AMEC's Process Development Service work?
faqs.answer: AMEC's Process Development Service provides expert support for developing and optimizing processes: 1) Recipe development - creating optimized process recipes for specific applications. 2) Process optimization - fine-tuning processes for performance, yield, and productivity. 3) Technology transfer - transferring processes from development to production. 4) Troubleshooting - diagnosing and resolving process issues. 5) Yield improvement - systematic improvement of manufacturing yield. Services can be provided at AMEC facilities or customer sites. The service includes knowledge transfer to customer personnel and comprehensive documentation. Process Development Service is available as standalone service or part of Integrated Fab Solution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Process Development Service accelerates process optimization and technology adoption. Available for all AMEC equipment.

faqs.question: What is the typical implementation timeline for AMEC solutions?
faqs.answer: Implementation timeline varies by solution scope: 1) Process Development Service - typically 2-6 months depending on complexity. 2) Integrated Fab Solution - typically 12-24 months from order to production including equipment build, installation, and process qualification. 3) Equipment-only solutions - typically 6-12 months including installation and acceptance. Timeline factors include: equipment configuration complexity, process development requirements, facility readiness, and customer resource availability. AMEC provides detailed project schedules and project management support. Early engagement in planning phase can help optimize timelines and identify potential issues.
faqs.decisionGuide: Timeline varies by scope. Early engagement helps optimize schedule and identify issues.

faqs.question: How does AMEC ensure solution performance meets requirements?
faqs.answer: AMEC ensures solution performance through: 1) Requirements definition - detailed documentation of performance requirements during project definition. 2) Factory acceptance testing - comprehensive testing at AMEC facilities before shipment. 3) Site acceptance testing - validation at customer site after installation. 4) Process qualification - demonstration of process performance against specifications. 5) Performance guarantees - contractual guarantees for key performance metrics. 6) Ongoing monitoring - continuous monitoring and optimization during production. Performance metrics typically include: throughput, yield, uptime, and process parameters. AMEC works collaboratively with customers to define appropriate metrics and acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Multi-level testing and guarantees ensure solution performance. Define metrics during project definition.

faqs.question: What ongoing support is provided after solution implementation?
faqs.answer: Ongoing support includes: 1) Technical support - phone and email support for operational questions. 2) Field service - on-site maintenance and troubleshooting support. 3) Spare parts - supply of spare parts for maintenance. 4) Process support - ongoing process optimization and troubleshooting. 5) Software updates - regular updates with improvements and new features. 6) Training updates - training for new personnel or new capabilities. Support can be provided under service contracts with defined response times or on-demand. Premium support options provide faster response for critical production applications. AMEC's local support team ensures responsive service.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive ongoing support ensures continued performance. Service contracts provide predictable costs.

solutions.title: Advanced Logic Manufacturing Solution
solutions.name: Advanced Logic Manufacturing Solution
solutions.description: Complete etching and deposition solution for advanced logic device manufacturing including FinFET and GAA transistor fabrication
solutions.longDescription: The Advanced Logic Manufacturing Solution from AMEC provides a comprehensive equipment and process solution for manufacturing advanced logic devices. This solution combines AMEC's leading etching and deposition equipment with expert process development and integration support. The solution supports technology nodes from 28nm down to 3nm and below, addressing the complex requirements of FinFET and Gate-All-Around transistor architectures. Key capabilities include multi-patterning support, atomic-level precision, and high selectivity processes required for advanced node manufacturing. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides local technical support and coordination throughout solution implementation and production.
benefits: Complete integrated solution reduces integration risk
Proven processes accelerate time to production

keyComponents.description: Advanced dielectric etching system for contact and via etching
Silicon etching system for fin and gate etching

coreAdvantages.title: Atomic-Level Precision
coreAdvantages.description: Advanced plasma control enables atomic-level precision for critical dimension control in advanced node devices

coreAdvantages.title: Multi-Patterning Support
coreAdvantages.description: Comprehensive support for SADP, SAQP, and other multi-patterning schemes required for sub-7nm devices

coreAdvantages.title: High Selectivity
coreAdvantages.description: Proprietary process chemistry delivers high selectivity for precise etch stop control

coreAdvantages.title: Production Proven
coreAdvantages.description: Qualified and in production at leading logic fabs worldwide

coreAdvantages.title: Integrated Support
coreAdvantages.description: Complete support from equipment to process optimization from AMEC and authorized distributor

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed to ramp 5nm production with stringent requirements for critical dimension control and defect density. Existing equipment had limitations in uniformity and selectivity for advanced multi-patterning processes.
customerCases.solution: AMEC provided a complete solution including 8 etching systems and 3 deposition systems. Our process team worked closely with the customer to develop optimized recipes for fin etching, gate etching, and contact etching. Extensive process matching ensured consistent results across all chambers.
customerCases.results: The customer achieved successful 5nm ramp with yield targets met within 6 months. CD uniformity improved by 25% compared to previous generation equipment. Defect density met aggressive targets for high-volume production. The customer has since expanded with additional AMEC equipment.
customerCases.feedback: AMEC's solution exceeded our expectations in both performance and support. The process development support was crucial for our rapid ramp.

customerCases.challenge: DRAM capacitor etching required extreme aspect ratios (>50:1) with straight profiles and minimal bowing. Existing equipment struggled with profile control and repeatability at these extreme dimensions.
customerCases.solution: AMEC deployed Primo SSC systems optimized for high aspect ratio etching. Custom process development addressed the specific requirements for capacitor etching. Chamber matching and process control algorithms ensured wafer-to-wafer consistency.
customerCases.results: The customer achieved >50:1 aspect ratios with straight profiles and <2% bowing. Yield improved by 15% compared to previous equipment. Production throughput met capacity targets. The solution is now standard for new DRAM generations.
customerCases.feedback: The process expertise from AMEC team made the difference. They understood our requirements and delivered optimized solutions.

faeInsights.insight: Advanced logic manufacturing presents unique challenges that require precise coordination between etching and deposition processes. The transition to FinFET and now GAA transistor architectures has dramatically increased the complexity of etch processes. Critical dimension control at the atomic level is essential, and any variation directly impacts device performance. My experience working with leading fabs has shown that success requires not just excellent equipment, but deep process expertise and close collaboration between equipment suppliers and device manufacturers. The key is understanding the interaction between patterning, etching, and deposition to achieve the precise three-dimensional structures required.
faeInsights.logic: The decision framework for advanced logic manufacturing solutions involves several key factors: First, assess the technology node requirements - 7nm, 5nm, and 3nm each have different challenges and equipment requirements. Second, evaluate the process flow complexity including the number of etch steps, multi-patterning requirements, and integration challenges. Third, consider the production volume and throughput requirements to determine equipment configuration. Fourth, assess the total cost of ownership including equipment cost, consumables, and maintenance. Finally, evaluate the supplier's track record and support capability for the specific technology node. AMEC's solution is particularly strong for customers seeking a balance of advanced capability and cost-effectiveness.
keyTakeaways: Atomic-level precision is essential for advanced node manufacturing
Multi-patterning support is critical for sub-7nm devices
Process integration expertise is as important as equipment capability
Chamber matching ensures consistent results across high-volume production
Total cost of ownership must include yield impact, not just equipment cost
commonPitfalls: Focusing only on equipment specifications without considering process integration
Underestimating the importance of chamber matching in multi-chamber configurations
Not planning for process development time in the project schedule
Overlooking the impact of consumables cost on total cost of ownership
bestPractices: Engage equipment supplier early in process development
Invest in thorough process matching before production ramp
Establish clear performance metrics and acceptance criteria
Plan for ongoing process optimization throughout production lifecycle

faqs.question: What technology nodes does this solution support?
faqs.answer: The Advanced Logic Manufacturing Solution supports technology nodes from 28nm down to 3nm and below. AMEC's Primo AD-RIE and Primo SSC systems are qualified and in production at leading fabs for 7nm, 5nm, and 3nm processes. The equipment features advanced plasma control, precise temperature management, and sophisticated process monitoring required for atomic-level precision. For nodes below 3nm, AMEC continues to develop next-generation equipment with enhanced capabilities. Contact our FAE team for specific node capability discussions and roadmap information.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC supports 3nm through 28nm nodes. Contact FAE for specific node requirements and roadmap.

faqs.question: How does AMEC handle multi-patterning requirements?
faqs.answer: AMEC's solution comprehensively supports multi-patterning schemes including SADP (Self-Aligned Double Patterning), SAQP (Self-Aligned Quadruple Patterning), and LELE (Litho-Etch-Litho-Etch). Key capabilities include: 1) Spacer etching with precise profile control for SADP/SAQP, 2) High selectivity to enable precise etch stop on mandrel layers, 3) Excellent CD uniformity for consistent spacer width, 4) Low defect density to prevent pattern defects, 5) Process control algorithms for consistent results across wafers. AMEC has extensive experience with multi-patterning processes at advanced nodes and provides proven recipes and process integration support.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC provides comprehensive multi-patterning support with proven processes at advanced nodes.

faqs.question: What is the typical chamber matching performance?
faqs.answer: AMEC specifies and achieves chamber-to-chamber matching within +/- 1% for critical parameters. Typical performance includes: 1) Etch rate matching within 1-2% across all chambers, 2) Uniformity matching within 0.5% variation, 3) Critical dimension matching within 2-3%, 4) Selectivity matching for consistent etch stop behavior. AMEC achieves this through precise hardware manufacturing, automated chamber qualification, and sophisticated matching algorithms. For high-volume manufacturing, excellent chamber matching enables load balancing and ensures consistent results regardless of which chamber processes the wafer. Factory acceptance testing includes chamber matching validation.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC achieves industry-leading chamber matching within +/- 1%. Essential for high-volume production.

faqs.question: How long does process development typically take?
faqs.answer: Process development timeline varies by complexity: 1) Simple process transfer - 2-4 weeks for adapting existing recipes to AMEC equipment, 2) New process development - 2-3 months for developing optimized recipes for new applications, 3) Complex integration - 3-6 months for multi-step processes with tight integration requirements, 4) Advanced node development - 6-12 months for cutting-edge processes requiring extensive optimization. AMEC provides process development support including recipe development, optimization, and troubleshooting. Timeline can be accelerated with close collaboration and clear requirements definition. Production ramp typically follows successful process qualification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Process development ranges from weeks to months depending on complexity. AMEC provides comprehensive development support.

faqs.question: What support is provided during production ramp?
faqs.answer: AMEC provides comprehensive production ramp support including: 1) On-site process engineers during initial production, 2) Real-time troubleshooting and optimization, 3) Yield analysis and improvement support, 4) Regular performance reviews and optimization, 5) Escalation support for critical issues, 6) Continuous improvement programs. Support can be provided under service contracts with defined response times and support levels. For critical production ramps, AMEC can provide dedicated on-site support teams. The goal is to achieve stable production as quickly as possible and maintain high performance throughout the production lifecycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive ramp support ensures successful production startup. Service contracts provide ongoing support.

faqs.question: How does AMEC ensure process stability over time?
faqs.answer: AMEC ensures long-term process stability through: 1) Robust hardware design with minimal drift, 2) Advanced process control with real-time monitoring and adjustment, 3) Predictive maintenance algorithms to prevent issues before they impact process, 4) Regular calibration and qualification procedures, 5) Chamber cleaning and conditioning protocols, 6) Statistical process control (SPC) for monitoring trends. AMEC systems are designed for high availability and consistent performance over years of production. Typical uptime exceeds 90% with proper maintenance. Process drift is minimized through hardware stability and automated control systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC systems designed for long-term stability with advanced process control and predictive maintenance.

solutions.title: 3D NAND Manufacturing Solution
solutions.name: 3D NAND Manufacturing Solution
solutions.description: High aspect ratio etching solution for 3D NAND memory manufacturing including channel hole and staircase etching
solutions.longDescription: The 3D NAND Manufacturing Solution from AMEC provides specialized etching capability for manufacturing 3D NAND memory devices. This solution addresses the unique challenges of etching extremely high aspect ratio features including channel holes and staircase structures. The solution supports aspect ratios exceeding 100:1 with excellent profile control and high selectivity to hard mask materials. Key capabilities include straight profile control, minimal bowing, and high productivity for cost-effective manufacturing. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides local technical support for solution implementation and ongoing production support.
benefits: Ultra-high aspect ratio capability for next-generation NAND
Excellent profile control minimizes device failures
Comprehensive support from implementation to production
features: High selectivity to oxide hard mask

keyComponents.description: High aspect ratio silicon etching system
Silicon etching system for standard applications
Dielectric etching for hard mask and oxide etching

coreAdvantages.title: Extreme Aspect Ratio Capability
coreAdvantages.description: Proven capability to etch channel holes with aspect ratios exceeding 100:1 while maintaining straight profiles

coreAdvantages.title: High Productivity
coreAdvantages.description: Fast etch rates and high throughput enable cost-effective production of 3D NAND devices

coreAdvantages.title: Excellent Profile Control
coreAdvantages.description: Advanced ICP source and process control enable precise profile control with minimal bowing

coreAdvantages.title: Production Proven
coreAdvantages.description: Qualified and in production at major NAND manufacturers worldwide

coreAdvantages.title: Hard Mask Compatibility
coreAdvantages.description: High selectivity to various hard mask materials enables deep etching with minimal mask erosion

customerCases.challenge: Channel hole etching for 128-layer NAND required etching >100:1 aspect ratio holes through alternating oxide/nitride stacks. Existing equipment had profile control issues with bowing and twist, causing device failures.
customerCases.solution: AMEC deployed Primo SSC-HD systems with custom process development for the specific stack structure. Optimized ICP source configuration and pulsed plasma process addressed profile control challenges. Extensive DOE optimized selectivity to oxide hard mask.
customerCases.results: Successfully achieved >100:1 aspect ratio with <2° taper and minimal bowing. Yield improved by 20% compared to previous equipment. Production throughput met capacity targets. Customer expanded deployment for next-generation 176-layer NAND.
customerCases.feedback: AMEC's solution enabled our 128-layer NAND production. The profile control capability is exceptional.

customerCases.challenge: Staircase etching for word line formation required precise control of step profile and critical dimension across hundreds of steps. Process uniformity was critical for device performance.
customerCases.solution: AMEC provided Primo SSC systems with optimized staircase etching processes. Advanced endpoint detection and process control ensured consistent step profiles. Chamber matching ensured uniform results across multiple systems.
customerCases.results: Achieved consistent staircase profiles with <5% variation across the wafer. Word line resistance uniformity improved significantly. Process is now qualified for production with high yield.
customerCases.feedback: The staircase etching solution exceeded our specifications. Excellent support from AMEC team throughout implementation.

faeInsights.insight: 3D NAND manufacturing represents one of the most challenging etching applications in semiconductor manufacturing. The transition from 2D to 3D NAND has pushed etching technology to its limits, with aspect ratios increasing from 10:1 to over 100:1 in just a few generations. The key challenge is not just achieving deep etching, but maintaining straight profiles and critical dimension control throughout the entire depth. My experience working with NAND manufacturers has shown that success requires a deep understanding of plasma physics, chemistry, and their interaction with complex material stacks. The hard mask strategy, plasma conditions, and process sequence must be carefully optimized together.
faeInsights.logic: The decision framework for 3D NAND etching solutions starts with understanding the specific layer stack and aspect ratio requirements. Different generations of 3D NAND have significantly different requirements - 64-layer, 128-layer, and 200+ layer devices each present unique challenges. The second consideration is productivity - etching deep structures takes time, so etch rate directly impacts cost per wafer. Third is profile control - bowing, twist, and taper must be minimized for device performance. Fourth is selectivity - the hard mask must survive the entire etch process. Finally, consider the supplier's experience with similar applications and their ability to provide process development support. AMEC's solution is particularly strong in high aspect ratio capability and productivity.
keyTakeaways: Aspect ratio requirements increase dramatically with each NAND generation
Profile control is as important as etch depth for device performance
Hard mask strategy is critical for high aspect ratio etching
Productivity is a major factor in cost per wafer for deep etching
Process development support is essential for optimizing complex etch processes
commonPitfalls: Underestimating the complexity of high aspect ratio etching process development
Not considering the interaction between hard mask and main etch processes
Focusing only on etch rate without considering profile control
Inadequate planning for chamber matching in high-volume production
bestPractices: Engage equipment supplier early in process development
Develop hard mask and main etch processes together as a system
Use design of experiments to optimize process windows
Plan for extensive characterization of profile and CD control

faqs.question: What is the maximum aspect ratio achievable with AMEC systems?
faqs.answer: AMEC's Primo SSC-HD system can achieve aspect ratios up to 150:1 for 3D NAND applications. This capability supports current and next-generation 3D NAND devices including 200+ layer stacks. The system maintains straight profiles with minimal bowing even at extreme aspect ratios. Actual achievable aspect ratio depends on the specific material stack, critical dimension, and process requirements. AMEC has demonstrated >100:1 aspect ratios in production at major NAND manufacturers. Contact our FAE team to discuss specific aspect ratio requirements for your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Primo SSC-HD supports up to 150:1 aspect ratios. Contact FAE for specific application requirements.

faqs.question: How is profile control maintained at high aspect ratios?
faqs.answer: Profile control at high aspect ratios is achieved through multiple techniques: 1) Advanced ICP source design provides independent control of plasma density and ion energy, 2) Pulsed plasma operation enables optimization of sidewall passivation, 3) Optimized gas chemistry balances etching and deposition for profile control, 4) Temperature control manages reaction rates and sidewall properties, 5) Process sequence optimization including soft landing and over-etch steps. AMEC's process development team has extensive experience optimizing profiles for 3D NAND applications. Typical results achieve <2° taper and minimal bowing even at >100:1 aspect ratios.
faqs.decisionGuide: Multiple techniques ensure profile control at extreme aspect ratios. AMEC has proven production experience.

faqs.question: What hard mask materials are compatible with AMEC etching?
faqs.answer: AMEC etching systems are compatible with various hard mask materials commonly used in 3D NAND: 1) Amorphous carbon - high selectivity and easy removal, 2) Silicon oxide - compatible with standard processes, 3) Silicon nitride - high selectivity for deep etching, 4) Metal hard masks - for extreme selectivity requirements. Selectivity to hard mask depends on the specific process chemistry and conditions. AMEC typically achieves >50:1 selectivity to oxide hard masks and >100:1 to carbon hard masks. Process development optimizes selectivity for the specific hard mask and etch depth requirements. Hard mask removal processes are also available for post-etch cleaning.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC supports various hard mask materials with high selectivity. Process optimized for specific hard mask requirements.

faqs.question: How does etch rate scale with aspect ratio?
faqs.answer: Etch rate typically decreases with increasing aspect ratio due to several factors: 1) Transport limitations - reactants and byproducts must travel deeper into high aspect ratio features, 2) Ion shadowing - ions may be shadowed from reaching the bottom of deep features, 3) Surface area - the bottom surface area decreases relative to sidewall area. AMEC systems mitigate these effects through: 1) High-density plasma sources for better transport, 2) Optimized pressure and flow for reactant delivery, 3) Process optimization for aspect ratio independent etching (ARIE). While some rate reduction is inevitable, AMEC processes maintain reasonable etch rates even at extreme aspect ratios. Productivity is optimized through process and hardware design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Etch rate decreases with aspect ratio but AMEC processes maintain productivity through optimization.

faqs.question: What is the typical process development timeline for new NAND generations?
faqs.answer: Process development timeline for new NAND generations: 1) Initial process development - 3-6 months for developing baseline processes for new stack structures, 2) Optimization - 2-4 months for fine-tuning profile control, selectivity, and uniformity, 3) Integration - 2-3 months for integrating with other process steps, 4) Qualification - 3-6 months for production qualification including reliability testing. Total timeline from initial development to production is typically 12-18 months for new NAND generations. AMEC provides process development support throughout this timeline. Early engagement allows AMEC to align equipment development with customer roadmaps.
faqs.decisionGuide: Typical development timeline 12-18 months for new NAND generations. Early engagement recommended.

faqs.question: How does AMEC support staircase etching applications?
faqs.answer: AMEC provides comprehensive support for staircase etching including: 1) Process development - optimized recipes for precise step profile control, 2) Endpoint detection - advanced endpoint systems for stopping at correct layers, 3) Profile control - maintaining consistent step profiles across hundreds of steps, 4) Uniformity optimization - ensuring consistent results across the wafer, 5) Chamber matching - critical for high-volume production. Staircase etching requires precise control of etch depth per step and consistent profile across all steps. AMEC's process control capabilities enable the precision required for word line formation. Production-proven processes are available for various NAND architectures.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC provides comprehensive staircase etching support with production-proven processes.
